title: Agentic Privacy Control Center
summary: DSC180 capstone project exploring privacy-aware analytical workflows through an agentic system design and a public artifact site documenting the project.
highlights: Developed a DSC180 capstone project centered on privacy-aware analytical workflows and agentic system design
Explored how privacy considerations shape data analysis, system behavior, and project outputs
Produced a public artifact site documenting the project structure, methodology, and deliverables
Built supporting materials that present the project clearly in a portfolio-ready format

title: Interactive MICE Dataset Visualization
summary: Interactive D3.js visualization exploring the MICE protein expression dataset through dynamic filtering and narrative analysis.
highlights: Built interactive D3 visualizations to explore biological protein expression data
Implemented dynamic filtering and exploratory views across experimental conditions
Designed a narrative interface to guide users through dataset patterns
Deployed the visualization as a public web application

title: NYC Citibike Product Analytics Pipeline
summary: Local-first analytics pipeline for 2025 NYC Citibike trip data, covering ingestion, warehouse modeling, SQL analysis, and notebook-based reporting.
highlights: Engineered an end-to-end local-first data pipeline ingesting 8.8 GB of official 2025 NYC Citibike trip archives from raw CSV extraction through DuckDB normalization, Parquet staging, and PostgreSQL loading
Designed a relational schema and authored analytics SQL using CTEs, window functions, date truncation, and percentile aggregations to answer core product questions
Built data quality validation checks against a curated schema and implemented rider-proxy metrics such as DAU, WAU, MAU, and churn proxies for datasets without persistent user identifiers
Authored a reproducible Jupyter notebook and reporting workflow demonstrating the analytics engineering process from ingestion to insight

title: The Relationship Between Income and Carbon Emissions: Do the Wealthy Pollute More in San Diego?
summary: COGS108 group project investigating whether income level is associated with higher carbon emissions in San Diego through data analysis and statistical testing.
highlights: Collaborated in a team to investigate the relationship between income and carbon emissions in San Diego
Performed data cleaning, exploratory analysis, and statistical hypothesis testing
Developed a reproducible notebook-based workflow to evaluate the research question
Produced a final project report communicating the analysis and findings

title: Local-First Clinical Documentation Assistant
summary: Privacy-focused AI application that generates structured therapy documentation from typed or dictated session content using fully local models.
highlights: Built a local-first clinical documentation system that converts session notes or dictation into structured therapy documents
Developed a conversational Chainlit interface combined with custom React components for audio capture, transcript editing, autosave, and document review
Implemented local speech-to-text transcription and LLM-based document generation while keeping processing on-device for privacy
Created structured workflows for generating progress notes, intake assessments, and treatment summaries
Built reliable DOCX template filling aligned with real clinical forms instead of freeform AI text
Added tooling for batch note generation from structured session data to test repeatable document creation workflows

links.note: Repository is private due to sensitive clinical data
